title: NOC 32101 – Licensed Practical Nurses | GTR Immigration
description: Licensed Practical Nurses (LPNs) under NOC 32101 are in strong and sustained demand across the healthcare system in Canada. Post-pandemic workforce shortages, an aging population, and expansion of long-term and community care services continue to drive employment growth in this TEER 2 occupation. With competitive wages, mandatory licensing that ensures professional recognition, and strong eligibility under economic immigration programs, NOC 32101 offers a practical pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3210 – Technical occupations in therapy and assessmentNOC Code32101

duties: Licensed Practical Nurses perform some or all of the following duties:
Provide nursing services within defined scope of practice
Conduct patient assessments and implement care plans
Take vital signs and apply aseptic techniques
Administer medications and document therapeutic effects
Evaluate patient progress and collaborate with healthcare teams
Provide health education to patients and families
Clean and sterilize operating rooms and instruments
employment_requirements: Completion of a vocational or college program for Licensed Practical Nurses
Mandatory registration with a provincial regulatory body in all provinces and territories
Successful completion of a registration examination
Additional academic training required for operating room technicians
British Columbia College of Nurses and Midwives
College and Association of Registered Nurses of Alberta
Ordre des infirmières et infirmiers auxiliaires du Québec
Internationally educated LPNs must complete credential assessment and pass provincial registration exams before practicing.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range between $42,400 and $65,300, depending on province, experience, and work setting.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32101 remains strong nationwide. Demand is driven by:
Increased healthcare access in rural and northern communities
Growth in community-based and home healthcare
Licensed Practical Nurses with Canadian registration have strong and stable employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Licensed Practical Nurses are employed in:
Nursing homes and long-term care facilities

This positive employment outlook reflects strong replacement demand and continued sector growth. High-demand provinces include Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, and northern regions such as Northwest Territories.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32101?
Provincial Nominee Program pathways targeting healthcare workers
Strong job security in public and private healthcare
Unionized salary structures in many provinces
Access to universal healthcare and settlement benefits
Healthcare occupations remain prioritized under Canada’s immigration system.
pathways: If you are a qualified Licensed Practical Nurse, NOC 32101 offers a practical and in-demand immigration pathway to Canada.
Eligibility assessment under Express Entry and PNP
